content: Thus hath the Lord God shewed unto me: and behold a basket of summer fruit.

And the songs of the temple shall be howlings in that day,
saith the Lord God:
there shall be many dead bodies in every place;
they shall cast them forth with silence.

Hear this, O ye that swallow up the needy,
even to make the poor of the land to fail,

saying, When will the new moon be gone, that we may sell corn?
and the sabbath, that we may set forth wheat,
making the ephah small, and the shekel great,
and falsifying the balances by deceit?

The Lord hath sworn by the excellency of Jacob,
Surely I will never forget any of their works.

Shall not the land tremble for this,
and every one mourn that dwelleth therein?
and it shall rise up wholly as a flood;
and it shall be cast out and drowned,
as by the flood of Egypt.

And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord God,
that I will cause the sun to go down at noon,
and I will darken the earth in the clear day:

Behold, the days come, saith the Lord God,
that I will send a famine in the land,
not a famine of bread, nor a thirst for water,
but of hearing the words of the Lord:

and they shall wander from sea to sea,
and from the north even to the east,
they shall run to and fro to seek the word of the Lord,
and shall not find it.
In that day shall the fair virgins and young men faint for thirst.
